title: RFK Jr. responds to uproar after singling out Dunkin’ for sugary drinks
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' for sugar content in drinks at the 'Eat Real Food' rally in Austin, Texas.
Gov. Maura Healey defended Dunkin', responding to Kennedy's critique with a meme referencing a Boston Globe article.
Boston.com poll shows a slim majority of 396 out of 770 readers agree with Kennedy's stance on sugary drinks.

title: Patriots sign veteran edge rusher to 1-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots signed linebacker Jesse Luketa to a one-year contract to enhance their defensive depth.
Jesse Luketa, 27, previously experienced a thigh injury but contributed three sacks with the Cardinals in 2024.
Luketa joins the Patriots as a possible replacement for pending free agent K'Lavon Chaisson in their pass-rushing unit.

title: Dismembered human remains found in Shirley identified as 69-year-old man
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Dismembered remains in Shirley identified as 69-year-old Peter Degan, last seen alive on Friday.
Degan was living in Rockland after serving time for cocaine trafficking; foul play suspected in his death.
Police and MSP recovered body parts at Maritime Veterans Memorial Bridge; investigation is ongoing.

title: ‘State-sanctioned cruelty’: Chelsea family sues ICE after Mother’s Day arrest last year
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Chelsea family sues ICE, alleging excessive force during Mother's Day arrest of Daniel Flores Martinez on May 11, 2025.
ICE agents reportedly smashed car window, arrested Martinez without explanation; incident left children traumatized, lawsuit claims.
Family alleges incident part of ICE's sanctioned practices; Department of Homeland Security, ICE have not commented.

title: In rare sightings, scientists spot blue whales in waters off Martha’s Vineyard
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Scientists from the New England Aquarium spotted blue whales off Martha's Vineyard, a rare occurrence in New England waters.
The sightings occurred on February 27 and 28, marking the first time the aquarium documented blue whales in this area.
Oceanographic conditions near southern New England are currently favorable for blue whales searching for food, according to experts.

title: One person found, one still missing after fishing boat overturns off Cape Cod
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Yankee Rose fishing boat overturned three nautical miles northeast of Race Point, Provincetown on Thursday.
The Coast Guard recovered one unconscious person and is still searching for a missing individual from the capsized vessel.
Last week, the Yankee Rose was involved in an emergency due to carbon monoxide poisoning of its crew.

title: Fenway location of Regina Pizzeria has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Regina Pizzeria's Fenway location at 1330 Boylston St. has closed as the lease wasn't renewed.
The Fenway pizzeria opened in 2013 and was part of Regina's expansion in Boston and Massachusetts.
Regina Pizzeria still operates 12 locations across Massachusetts, mostly quick-service, including the historic North End restaurant.

title: Mansfield man killed in crash on I-95 in Attleboro, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chad Askew, 43, from Mansfield died after his Honda Civic struck a tractor-trailer on I-95 North in Attleboro.
The crash near Exit 4 on I-95 was reported around 5:20 a.m.; Askew died at Sturdy Memorial Hospital.
Massachusetts State Police issued an alert for a man seen fleeing the crash scene; investigation is ongoing.

title: Newly released video shows crash that left pickup truck dangling over Zakim Bridge
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MassDOT released video of a crash involving a pickup truck dangling over the Zakim Bridge.
The crash occurred February 5 after the driver swerved to avoid a disabled vehicle, police stated.
A snowbank and fence prevented the truck from going over the edge; no injuries were reported.

title: A Mass. college is buying a local golf course. Here’s why.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Stonehill College is purchasing Easton Country Club, a 150-acre golf course in Easton, for athletics and academics.
The golf course will host Stonehill's women's golf team and a new men's program for the 2027-28 NCAA Division I season.
The acquisition supports Stonehill's strategic plan, enhancing student academics, recreation, and community engagement while continuing current operations.
